items.title: Prime Day pricing is still live on Samsung’s 27-inch QHD 180Hz QD-OLED gaming monitor at $340 (All-time low)
items.summary: Over at Amazon, you’ll find Samsung’s Odyssey G50SF 27-inch QHD QD-OLED gaming monitor currently sitting at $339.95 shipped. This is originally a $550 unit that’s been fetching $500 at Amazon lately. It’s now seeing a 32% markdown on that price, shaving $160 off its usual going rate. This is essentially the Prime Day price that went live last week, and it’s now $10 below its previous all-time low. This is one of the more affordable QD-OLED monitors out there, which is now going for even less. It’s hard to go wrong with this display if you’re looking to pick up a QD-OLED monitor for your battlestation, and you can learn more about it below.

items.title: Samsung’s officially licensed 256GB Switch 2 Express microSD hits lowest price at just $39
items.summary: Amazon is now offering Samsung’s officially licensed Switch 2-compatible 256GB Mario-themed Express microSD card for $39 shipped. This model launched alongside the Switch 2 last year, as one of the first Express microSD cards for the handheld. We saw it drop to the $39 mark when it was discounted for Mario Day earlier this year, and today’s offer lands it back to that lowest price with a 35% discount. Walmart and Best Buy are also matching the offer, giving you a few options in case Amazon sells out. This is a great offer for anyone who missed the Prime Day deal on Samsung’s P9 Express card at $40. You are getting the same 256GB capacity and Switch 2-ready Express storage performance here for a dollar less, and with the officially licensed Mario design. Head below for a closer look.

items.summary: Hot on the heels of its Tech Fest Prime Day sale, Best Buy is wasting no time kicking off its 4th of July event with major discounts on Apple gear, Android phones, TVs, and more. We’re also tracking solid offers on Windows laptops and desktops, including the latest Copilot+ notebooks powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon X2 Plus, AMD Ryzen AI 400-series, and Intel Core Ultra Series 3 “Panther Lake” chips. While some of these models were discounted during last week’s Tech Fest Prime Day sale, Best Buy has now introduced a fresh batch of laptop deals starting at $329.99 shipped. Prime Day was not particularly big for Windows laptop deals, so you might want to jump in and check out offers at Best Buy now to lock in some of the lowest prices on these latest Windows notebooks.

That said, we did get the very first price drop on AirTag 2 for this year’s Prime Day festivities and that deal is still live today on both the single AirTag listing and the 4-pack.

items.title: Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in the Palisades fire trial
items.summary: Jonathan Rinderknecht was facing arson charges for setting a fire on New Year's Day in 2025, which became one of the deadliest wildfires in LA history. To make their case, prosecutors turned to location data from his iPhone, security camera footage, and witness testimony. But they also turned to his ChatGPT logs. Prosecutors said that […]

items.title: 9to5Toys News Weekly recap – Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders and details, the last of the summer sales, more
items.summary: Welcome to 9to5Toys News Weekly – your go-to spot to catch up on the latest new product releases, features, and hands-on reviews. This week it has been all about Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders, the GTA VI code-in-a-box debacle, the biggest sales of the year. Alongside some hangover Prime Day deals you can still score right now, today is also your last chance as the massive summer sale at Walmart alongside the final 24 hours of the Best Buy Tech Fest deals.

items.title: Ends tonight: Last chance at up to 50% off ‘must-have’ gear in Best Buy’s giant Tech Fest sale
items.summary: Just after Prime Day wrapped up late Friday night, today will we your last chance to get in on the massive Best Buy Tech Fest sale. Its biggest sale of the year is still rolling for one last 24-hour stretch before the whole thing closes down later tonight.
